prune.volumesConfirm: All unused volumes and their data will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
prune.networksConfirm: All unused networks will be removed. This action cannot be undone.

sites.createDescription: Create a new Nginx website. A virtual host configuration will be generated automatically.

sites.sslDescription: Obtain a Let's Encrypt SSL certificate for {{name}}. This may take a moment while the certificate is issued.
sites.sslEmailHelp: Used for certificate expiry notifications from Let's Encrypt.
sites.empty: No sites found. Create your first site to get started.

notEnabled.description: Enable cluster mode to manage 2-32 nodes as a unified cluster.

init.advertiseAddressDesc: The IP address other nodes will use to reach this server.

overview.confirmDisband: Are you sure you want to disband the cluster? This node will switch to standalone mode.

tokens.generateDescription: Create a single-use token for adding a new node to the cluster

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your account and system settings
settings.twoFADescription: Add an extra layer of security with a TOTP authenticator app
settings.scanQR: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app, or enter the secret manually:
settings.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
settings.terminalTimeoutHint: Time before idle terminal sessions are closed. Set to 0 for no timeout.
settings.invalidTimeout: Please enter a valid number (0 or greater)
settings.maxUploadSizeHint: Maximum allowed size for file uploads. Default is 1024 MB (1 GB).
settings.invalidMaxUploadSize: Please enter a number greater than 0
settings.updateDescription: Check for updates and install the latest version
settings.backupDescription: Download a backup of your database, configuration, and Compose projects
settings.backupItemDB: Admin account, sessions, settings, compose projects, custom log sources
settings.backupItemConfig: Server port, JWT secret, DB path, Docker socket, log settings
settings.backupDockerDataNote: Docker data (volumes, images, containers) is NOT included in the backup
settings.restoreDescription: Upload a backup file to restore database and configuration
settings.restoreConfirm: This will replace your current database and configuration. The service will restart. Continue?
settings.updateConfirm: The panel will be updated and the service will restart. Continue?

tuning.resetConfirm: Reset all tuning settings to system defaults?

catDesc.security: SYN flood protection, IP spoofing prevention, redirect blocking

tuning.benefit_network: Benefits: BBR improves throughput by 10-40%, larger TCP buffers speed up bulk transfers, Fast Open reduces connection latency, optimized keepalive prevents idle connection waste
tuning.caution_network: Caution: BBR requires kernel 4.9+ (Ubuntu 24.04 supported). tcp_tw_reuse may rarely cause issues behind NAT. Verify network services after applying
tuning.benefit_memory: Benefits: Lower swappiness reduces disk I/O and improves responsiveness, optimized dirty ratios prevent sudden I/O bursts, lower cache pressure improves filesystem cache efficiency
tuning.caution_memory: Caution: Very low swappiness on low-RAM systems (< 4GB) may trigger OOM Killer. High min_free_kbytes reduces available memory. Use Reset to Defaults to revert instantly
tuning.benefit_filesystem: Benefits: Higher file-max enables massive concurrent connections, more inotify watches prevent IDE/build tool errors, increased AIO improves database performance
tuning.caution_filesystem: Caution: Very high file-max slightly increases memory usage. Minimal side effects for typical servers. Process-level ulimits may also need adjustment
tuning.benefit_security: Benefits: Protects against SYN flood and Smurf attacks, blocks IP spoofing, disables ICMP redirects to prevent MITM attacks, blocks source routing manipulation
tuning.caution_security: Caution: All values are safe for standard server operations. Multi-homed (multi-NIC router) setups should verify rp_filter and send_redirects. No side effects for typical servers
tuning.rollbackHint: If the system becomes unreachable, changes will be automatically reverted

files.title: File Manager
files.name: Name
files.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

cron.title: Cron Jobs
cron.subtitle: Manage system scheduled tasks
cron.guideWhat: A Linux task scheduler that automatically runs commands at specified times.
cron.guideWho: This panel manages the root user's crontab. All registered commands run with root privileges.
cron.guideHow: The cron daemon checks the crontab every minute in the background and executes tasks when the schedule matches.
cron.guideScheduleDesc: min(0-59) hour(0-23) day(1-31) month(1-12) weekday(0-7, 0 and 7 = Sunday)
cron.createDescription: Add a new scheduled task to the system crontab
cron.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this cron job? This action cannot be undone.

network.title: Network / VPN
network.subtitle: Manage network interfaces, VPN, DNS, and routing
network.applyWarningDesc: Applying network configuration may temporarily disconnect your connection. Continue?
network.ipAddressesHint: One address per line in CIDR format (e.g. 192.168.1.100/24)
network.applyConfigCaution: Network may be temporarily unavailable while changes are applied. Make sure you have alternative access.

wireguard.notInstalledDesc: A fast and modern VPN protocol. Install to manage VPN connections.
wireguard.noInterfacesDesc: Add a configuration file to create a VPN connection.
wireguard.configNameHint: Start with letter, alphanumeric/hyphen/underscore, max 15 chars (e.g. wg0)
wireguard.editConfigHint: PrivateKey is masked. Enter a new key to change it.
wireguard.maskedKeyError: Please replace masked keys (********) with real keys before saving.
wireguard.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}"? If active, it will be brought down first.

tailscale.notInstalledDesc: A mesh VPN that securely connects your devices. Install to get started.
tailscale.notConnectedDesc: Connect to Tailscale to join your network.
tailscale.needsLogin: Authentication required. Enter an Auth Key or connect to get an auth URL.
tailscale.authKeyHint: Auth key from the Tailscale admin console. Leave empty for browser authentication.
tailscale.logoutConfirm: Logging out will remove this device from the tailnet. Continue?
tailscale.acceptRoutesDesc: Accept subnet routes advertised by other devices
tailscale.acceptRoutesHint: Routes must be approved in the admin console first.
tailscale.advertiseExitNodeDesc: Allow other devices to use this device as an exit node
tailscale.advertiseExitNodeHint: Requires approval in the admin console after enabling.
tailscale.selectExitNodeDesc: All traffic will be routed through the selected device

logs.title: Log Viewer
logs.subtitle: View and stream system and application logs
logs.selectSourcePrompt: Select a log source from the sidebar to view its contents
logs.selectSourceFirst: Please select a log source first
logs.deleteSourceConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this custom log source?
logs.pathInvalid: Please enter a valid absolute path
logs.guideTitle: How to use the Log Viewer
logs.guideStep1Desc: Choose from system logs, firewall, Fail2ban, and more in the sidebar. Use the + button to add custom log sources.
logs.guideStep2Desc: Firewall, Auth, Fail2ban, and SFPanel logs are auto-parsed into structured tables. Press Ctrl+F to search and highlight matches.
logs.guideStep3Desc: Click the Live button to stream new log entries in real-time via WebSocket. Enable auto-scroll to follow the latest entries.

swap.title: Swap Management
swap.name: Name
swap.resizeDescription: Change the size of the swap file. The swap will be temporarily disabled during resize.
swap.resizeHint: Swap will be temporarily disabled during the resize operation.
swap.swapoffWarning: Not enough free RAM to safely disable swap. Data in swap exceeds available RAM — this may cause the system to freeze.
swap.exceedsDisk: The selected size exceeds available disk space.
swap.removeConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove swap {{name}}?

status.title: Firewall Status
status.enableConfirm: Are you sure you want to enable the firewall?
status.disableConfirm: Are you sure you want to disable the firewall? Your server will be unprotected.

rules.title: Firewall Rules
rules.editDescription: Edit rule #{{number}}. The existing rule will be deleted and a new rule will be added.
rules.editDeleteFailed: New rule was added but failed to delete the old rule. Please delete it manually.
rules.editAddFailed: Old rule was deleted but failed to add the new rule
rules.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete rule #{{number}}?
rules.fromIPHint: Leave empty to allow all IPs. e.g. 192.168.1.100, 10.0.0.0/24, 2001:db8::/32

fail2ban.title: Fail2ban
fail2ban.notInstalledDesc: A tool to protect your server from brute-force attacks.
fail2ban.aboutDesc: Fail2ban monitors log files for repeated authentication failures and automatically bans the offending IPs — an intrusion prevention tool.
fail2ban.aboutHow1: Monitors service log files (SSH, Nginx, etc.) in real-time
fail2ban.aboutHow2: Automatically bans IPs after a configured number of auth failures
fail2ban.aboutHow3: Automatically unbans after a set duration (configurable)
fail2ban.aboutJailSSH: SSH brute-force protection (most important, enabled by default)
fail2ban.aboutJailRecidive: Long-term ban for repeat offenders across jails
fail2ban.aboutRecommend: Typically, enabling just the sshd jail significantly improves your server's security.
fail2ban.name: Name
fail2ban.unbanConfirm: Are you sure you want to unban {{ip}}?
fail2ban.editConfigDesc: Change the ban policy for this Fail2ban jail.
fail2ban.configWarning: Changes take effect immediately. Incorrect settings may block legitimate access — proceed with caution!
fail2ban.maxRetryHint: Number of auth failures before banning (1–100)
fail2ban.findTimeHint: Window in which max retries are counted before ban. In seconds.
fail2ban.addJailDesc: Select a template or create a custom Fail2ban jail.
fail2ban.customJailDesc: Manually configure filter and log path
fail2ban.jailNameHint: Letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores only
fail2ban.deleteJailConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete jail "{{name}}"? All bans and configuration will be removed.

docker.title: Docker Port Firewall
docker.description: Control external access to Docker published ports
docker.addRuleDesc: Add an iptables rule for Docker published ports
docker.noDockerUserChain: DOCKER-USER chain not found. Make sure Docker is running.

logs.title: Firewall Logs
logs.description: View UFW and Fail2ban logs in real-time

appStore.title: App Store
appStore.subtitle: Install self-hosted apps with one click
appStore.guideTitle: How does the App Store work?
appStore.guideStep1Desc: Choose an app and configure environment variables like ports and passwords. Advanced mode lets you edit docker-compose.yml and .env directly.
appStore.guideStep2Desc: Generates docker-compose.yml and .env files from your settings, then automatically pulls images and starts containers via Docker Compose.
appStore.guideStep3Desc: Installed apps appear in Docker Stacks where you can start, stop, restart, or remove them just like any other Compose stack.
